transcript.text:
A very popular ramen spot with 84 years of tradition
The owner traveled to learn the recipe
It's finally here! Bon appetit
He went over to Japan to learn the ways of ramen
[Japanese ramen in Korea]
Hello everyone
I'm at Hongdae today to eat ramen
Ramen from HopeKen Honpo (ホープ軒本舗 吉祥寺店), Japan
This place holds 84 years of tradition
The owner here went over to learn the recipe
So we can taste the true flavors of ramen here
Let's check it out
[Seonghwa Ramen] [Seoul, Mapo-gu]
Hello
There are 4 types of ramen here
They have gyoza (fried dumplings) and chashu too (braised pork belly)

I'm ready to order
One of every ramen
Yaki gyoza (handmade)
And chashu, please
-Did you make a trip out there to learn? -Yes, I did
That place is well-known Passed down for 3 generations and with 84 years of tradition
-Does it taste the same? -Yes
The owner of that place used to be the very first ramen owner here in South Korea about 25 years ago
It was way ahead of time
He temporarily closed the restaurant, and I took over 7 years ago
Japanese broth, noodles You name it
[Very excited!]
[Getting the seasoning ready]
[Salted shrimp?]
[Can't wait]
[Ramen with 84 years of tradition]

[4 different bowls of ramen]
Thank you
Tonkotsu Ramen, Shoyu Ramen,
Shio Ramen, Miso Ramen
[Different colored bowls]
That's all the ramen
What's the difference?
Tonkotsu is pork broth
Shoyu is chicken broth with soy sauce
Shio is salted shrimp
Miso is soybean paste
It's going to be great!
Let's try the broth first
Tonkotsu is the original, right?
84 years of tradition...
This is different from the Tonkotsu broth I know

The soup is great
The broth is full of flavor
And clean at the same time
Miso
[Wait for chashu and gyoza!]
[This is not your everyday chashu
[Handmade gyoza as well]
All here Bon appetit
Tonkotsu ramen first
This is very different from what I'm used to
It's not as thick as bone broth
It's very clean and light
A little greasy
I'll try the chashu too
Chewy and delicious
More on the chewy side

Gyoza
Gotta eat it while it's hot
I saw you from YouTube here and there
Thank you for the food!
Some soy sauce
[She's really good at eating....]
I saw her eating a lot of food
[Not eating as much today]
-Tzuyang, right? -Yes
-Chinese? -No, 100% Korean
Weren't you Chinese?
-Since you were a K-pop star? -K-pop star?!
I was?
[It's okay! It happens..!!]
Shio next
The broth is made with salted shrimp

This is also very clean
Not greasy
This should go well with kimchi
Do they have pickled radish?
Thank you
It's aged kimchi, my favorite
Put kimchi on top
I don't think I've had Japanese ramen with kimchi before
Let's eat it here
Should be good with chashu too
What's this? I guess you put this on top of the meat?
It tastes so good together
Let's have 1 more slice
It's like a mixture of every seasoning paste there is
Salty and tasty
A strong soybean taste

Chashu on top
This taste much more like a Korean dish Like a Korean-style ramen
Not too much seasoning
Gotta have boiled eggs
This is huge
I thought the Japanese-style tonkotsu ramen would be very different from Korean tonkotsu ramen
But this feels even more Korean
Another bite
With egg
It fell out
This was like a Korean dish But this feels more like ramen
Tonkotsu is the best
Pork skin is on it too
So it's chewy
[Miso ramen next]
[Pork broth with soybean paste]

This is just like soybean stew
Would you like some spiced scallions?
Just a little bit
A little? Okay
Ramen noodles need to be eaten with their broth
Can have the full experience just with the noodles
Get some soup and put the noodles on top
It's great like this
Thank you This is spiced scallions?
Thank you
Dried seaweed too
This dish is also at HopeKen Honpo
This tasted like a Korean ramen dish Is it because of the salted shrimp?
They use salted shrimp in Japan too
Soybean paste as well
All of the ingredients here are directly from Japan

-Wasn't it hard to learn? -Of course it was
I had to learn everything from the basics
And he didn't teach me everything
And when he approves it, that's when you can say you fully learned it, right?
There's a difference though The difference in water between Japan and Korea
The water tastes different in 2 countries
You went through a lot
I cried a lot
The spiced scallion chashu is for drinking
Also great with ramen and dried seaweed
Thank you!
There's chashu in here
And scallions
Yummy!
This is really a drinking dish
The texture of this dish calls for alcohol

Very similar to spiced cockle
Put it on dried seaweed
I ate too much
Small pieces of chashu inside
This would be good with rice too
Shoyu
Dunk it
I can definitely taste the soy sauce
Tonkotsu was my favorite
Then Miso Then Shoyu
[How about me...?]
Delicious Goes really well together
Really nice
Some shoyu broth
Dummy
Why am I like this?

I finished the spiced scallions already
Only chashu left
[She's almost done...?]
1 last piece
That was great
I'm finished!
[omg]
[My Lord...]
But that's what you're famous for
[Thank you for the meal!] [Subtitles by Anthony Hyun Choi] [antchoi91@gmail.com]
